editorPanel.allowDynamicArmyHelp: When enabled, players bring their own army based on the matchmaking battle type and deploy it inside their zones. When disabled, the scenario's pre-placed units define the full roster (army composition is skipped).
editorPanel.noInherentAmmoHelp: When enabled, units with an ammo system spawn with no ammo of their own and draw their whole supply from the shared reserve, so they can't fire on the first turn and only open up once the reserve resupplies them.
editorPanel.goBackConfirm: Are you sure you want to go back and exit the editor?

formationModal.title: Edit custom formation
formationModal.collisionHelp: Footprint shape: a circle (radius) or a rectangle (frontage across the front, depth front-to-back). Use radius 0 for no collision (flying / ghost).
formationModal.fireEdgesHelp: Each fire edge emits along one face of the rectangle. A formation with no fire edges fires a default single front edge; circle formations do not fire.
formationModal.errIdInUse: Formation id "{{id}}" is already in use
formationModal.errEdgeIndex: Fire edge {{n}}: edge index must be 0-3
formationModal.errEdgeArc: Fire edge {{n}}: arc must be between 0 and 360
formationModal.errEdgeEmitters: Fire edge {{n}}: emitters must be a positive integer

unitTemplateModal.title: Edit custom unit template
unitTemplateModal.reportStatsHelp: Tally each unit contributes to battle reports (e.g. men, horses, guns). Keys come from the era's built-in units.

errors.idInUse: Unit category id "{{id}}" is already in use
errors.allowedOrderRequired: All allowed-order entries must be selected
errors.allowedOrderUnknown: Allowed order "{{name}}" is not a known order for this era

chargeBacklash.description: Multiplier on the counter-hit this category deals back to a charger (higher = braces and hits back harder, 0 = none). The after-running value applies when the defender has already run and can't brace; it falls back to the base multiplier. Defaults to 1.

chargeStamina.description: Max stamina a charge drains from this category. The charger pays it in full head-on and less when flanking; a defender pays it in full when flanked, with a 25% floor either way. Defaults to 0 (no cost).

resistances.description: Per-damage-type resistance modifier (negative = take more damage, positive = take less).

autofire.description: Priority weight per target unit category. Higher values are preferred targets for autofire.
autofire.warnOnMaxHint: Shows an ammo-waste warning on the Max tier (e.g. artillery).

errors.idInUse: Damage type id {{id}} is already in use
errors.nameInUse: Damage type name "{{name}}" is already in use
errors.idBelongsToBuiltIn: Damage type id {{id}} belongs to built-in "{{name}}". Keep the name to override it, or pick a different id.
errors.nameBelongsToBuiltIn: Name "{{name}}" belongs to built-in id {{id}}. Set id to {{id}} to override it, or pick a different name.
errors.rangedNeedsBracket: Ranged damage types need at least one range bracket
errors.maxRangeRequired: Max range must be greater than 0
errors.rangeBandBounds: Each band's From/To must be within 0–100%, with From ≤ To.
errors.lastBandMustReachMax: The last band must reach 100% (max range).

targetStat.helper: In % of the target's stat. No effect above 'from'; full 'value' at or below 'to'. Grows as the target's HP/org drops.

terrainCategoryModal.title: Edit custom terrain category
terrainCategoryModal.identityHint: Overrides the era's terrain category config for this scenario. The id is fixed to a built-in category - terrains keep their category assignments, you only adjust the modifiers.
terrainCategoryModal.perUnitCategoryHint: Keys are unit category ids. Use the `*` wildcard to set a default applied to all categories that don't have an explicit entry.

errors.idInUse: Terrain category id "{{id}}" is already in use

spritesPanel.title: Custom sprites
spritesPanel.description: Upload your own sprites here, then pick them in a unit's formations. They are embedded in the scenario (max {{kb}}KB each).
spritesPanel.maxSpritesAlert: You can upload at most {{count}} custom sprites.
spritesPanel.tooLargeAlert: Image too large; could not compress under {{kb}}KB. Try a smaller or simpler image.
spritesPanel.invalidImageAlert: Could not read the image. Use a PNG or WebP file.
spritesPanel.deleteBlockedAlert: Can't delete: still used by {{where}}. Change those formations first.
spritesPanel.libraryDescription: Browse the era's built-in sprites for inspiration.

unitTemplatesPanel.title: Custom unit templates
unitTemplatesPanel.description: Define new unit templates with custom stats, formations, and damage types. Use "Override" to replace a built-in for this scenario (useful for balance testing) or "Clone" to add a fresh template with id >= {{minId}}.
unitTemplatesPanel.deleteBlocked: Can't delete "{{name}}" - still used by {{refs}}. Remove those units first.

formationsPanel.title: Custom unit formations
formationsPanel.description: Define new formation templates (collision, modifiers, shooting rules) that custom units can use.
formationsPanel.deleteBlocked: Can't delete "{{id}}" - still used by {{refs}}. Remove those references first.

unitCategoriesPanel.title: Custom unit categories
unitCategoriesPanel.description: Define new unit categories (collision levels, deployment section, allowed orders, routing behavior). Referenced by custom units via their category field.
unitCategoriesPanel.deleteBlocked: Can't delete "{{id}}" - still used by {{refs}}. Remove those references first.

damageTypesPanel.title: Custom damage types
damageTypesPanel.description: Define new melee or ranged damage types. Referenced by custom units via meleeDamageType or rangedDamageTypes.
damageTypesPanel.deleteBlocked: Can't delete "{{name}}" - still used by {{refs}}. Remove those references first.

terrainCategoriesPanel.title: Custom terrain categories
terrainCategoriesPanel.description: Override terrain category configs for this scenario. Use this to give custom unit categories specific terrain modifiers, or to introduce new terrain category ids.
terrainCategoriesPanel.noOverrides: No custom terrain category overrides yet.

overrideEditor.structuralTooltip: Structural / determinism constant. Changing it can break rendering, replays, or matchmaking.

importModal.title: Import {{kind}} from scenario
importModal.pickFilePrompt: Pick a scenario file (the JSON the editor's Download button produces) to import its custom {{kind}} from.
importModal.fileReadError: Couldn't read that file as a scenario.
importModal.invalidForEra: This scenario's custom definitions aren't valid for the current era (e.g. {{message}}).
importModal.importWouldInvalidate: Import would make the scenario invalid (e.g. {{message}}). Nothing was changed.

importOverridesModal.title: Import {{target}} from scenario
importOverridesModal.pickFilePrompt: Pick a scenario file (the JSON the editor's Download button produces) to import its {{target}} from.

gameConstants.title: Game constant overrides
gameConstants.description: Override this era's numeric constants for this scenario. Only changed values are saved; Reset returns a field to the era default. Keys marked ⚠ are structural - changing them can break rendering, replays, or matchmaking. Values are raw internal units.

gameRules.title: Game rule overrides
gameRules.description: Override this era's gameplay rules for this scenario. Only changed values are saved; use Reset to return a field to the era default. Values are raw internal units (some stats are stored pre-scaled).

ordersPanel.title: Order overrides
ordersPanel.description: Override the values of this era's built-in orders for this scenario. Only changed values are saved; use Reset to return a field to the era default. You can modify existing orders but not add new ones.

clonePicker.alreadyOverriddenTooltip: This built-in already has a custom override in this scenario; edit it from the list instead.

scale.errorInvalid: Please enter valid positive numbers for width and height in tiles
scale.warningBody: Make sure you have saved your scenario before proceeding. Resizing will scale all terrains, heights, units, objectives, and trigger positions.

expandCut.intro: Positive values add tiles, negative values remove tiles from each side.
expandCut.errorTooSmall: Cannot resize: resulting map would be smaller than 1×1 tiles. Please adjust the values.
expandCut.errorTooLarge: Cannot resize: resulting map would exceed maximum size of {{maxWidth}}×{{maxHeight}} tiles.
expandCut.warningBody: Make sure you have saved your scenario before proceeding. Expanding/cutting will add or remove tiles from the specified sides. Units and objectives outside the new bounds will be removed.

localesPanel.title: Locales
localesPanel.description: Locales are used to translate the scenario into different languages.
localesPanel.noKeys: No translation keys. Add a key to get started.

randomMap.title: Random Map
randomMap.moreInfo: More info about random maps here
randomMap.proceduralExplanation: These instructions generate the map from a new seed each match; the saved scenario drops its baked map. The map shown here is only a preview. Objectives placed by Objective / Objective layer instructions appear at match time, not in this preview.
randomMap.noInstructions: No instructions yet. Add one above or load a template.
randomMap.switchToPresetConfirm: Switching to a preset keeps the current terrain and clears the generation instructions. Continue?
randomMap.replaceInstructionsConfirm: Replace the current instructions with the template's?
randomMap.fillFromTemplateDescription: Fill the current map's terrain from a procedural template. This bakes a fixed (preset) map and does not keep any generation instructions.
randomMap.confirmOverwriteLabel: I understand this will overwrite all terrain
randomMap.importMustBeProcedural: The imported file must be a procedural template (no baked map, with generation instructions).
randomMap.invalidJsonFile: Invalid JSON file. Please check the file format.
randomMap.confirmOverwriteWarning: Please confirm that you understand this will overwrite the terrain.
randomMap.selectedNotProcedural: Selected scenario is not a procedural template.

randomDeployment.title: Deployment zones
randomDeployment.explanation: Where each team's units start, as percentages of the map. Keep it symmetric for a fair match, or uncheck to author each side.
randomDeployment.noZones: No zones — the generator uses defaults.

scoring.heading: VP weights (scenario override)
scoring.hint: Leave a field empty to inherit the battle-type or era default.
scoring.basePointsInfo: Starting VP both teams get before casualties, objectives, or pressure are applied.
scoring.casualtiesInfo: Max VP from the casualty difference between teams. Higher favours attrition over holding objectives.
scoring.underPressureInfo: VP a team loses per turn while under pressure. 0 disables the penalty.
scoring.pressureThresholdInfo: A team is under pressure while it holds this % of objective VP or less.
scoring.bigDefaultInfo: Fallback VP for a big objective that doesn't set its own value. Individual objectives can still override it.
scoring.smallDefaultInfo: Fallback VP for a small objective that doesn't set its own value. Individual objectives can still override it.
scoring.tieBreakInfo: On the final turn, a team trailing on VP by more than this is treated as defeated.

objectiveZones.heading: Objective zones (scenario override)
objectiveZones.hint: How much to shrink the placeable-objective zones. Leave a field empty to inherit the battle-type or era default.
objectiveZones.smallInsetInfo: How much to narrow the small-objective zone on each side. Trims only its width, keeping its vertical reach.
objectiveZones.bigInsetInfo: How much to shrink the big-objective box on each side, centered inside the deployment zone.
